claims.claim: On 19 April 2026, the four Undangs of Negeri Sembilan declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the proposed 12th Ruler.
claims.source: BERNAMA report carried by NST, 20 Apr 2026, reporting MB Aminuddin Harun's statement: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/04/1421666/declaration-depose-negri-sembilan-ruler-not-recognised-says-mb ; Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026, reporting the declarations and live-streamed reading: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
claims.correction: Use 'purportedly declared' or 'announced a declaration' rather than implying legal effect. The state government did not recognise the declaration.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue found as of 17 May 2026.

claims.claim: State government rejects it: one Undang was dismissed in May 2025.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810 ; BERNAMA Malay version, 19/20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/bm/news.php?id=2546809
claims.correction: The more precise formulation is: the Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar said Mubarak's dismissal took effect on 13 May 2025 and that the DKU special sitting on 17 April 2026 accepted/advised acceptance of that dismissal. The other Undangs have disputed this.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue found; however, later court proceedings filed on 5 May 2026 challenge access to the DKU minutes.

claims.claim: Four Undangs declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the 12th Ruler.
claims.source: BERNAMA/NST, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/04/1421666/declaration-depose-negri-sembilan-ruler-not-recognised-says-mb ; Malay Mail legal explainer, 5 May 2026: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741
claims.correction: It should say they purported to depose Tuanku Muhriz and named/proclaimed Tunku Nadzaruddin, because the state government and several constitutional experts say the attempt had no legal effect.

claims.claim: Three hold office; one was dismissed 11 months earlier.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810 ; The Star, 21 Apr 2026, reporting the four Undangs' dispute of the MB's claim: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/04/21/four-undang-dispute-mbs-claim-over-mubaraks-removal
claims.correction: State as attributed: 'The state government says Mubarak had been dismissed effective 13 May 2025; the four Undangs disputed the MB's account.' The '11 months earlier' calculation is broadly correct from 13 May 2025 to 19 Apr 2026.

claims.claim: The same Undang who proclaimed Muhriz in 2008 signed this one too.
claims.source: The Star, 29 Dec 2008, says Tuanku Muhriz was proclaimed by Undang Luak Sungai Ujong Datuk Klana Petra Mubarak Dohak: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2008/12/29/tunku-mukhriz-proclaimed-new-ruler-of-negri-sembilan-updated ; BERNAMA/NST, 20 Apr 2026, identifies Datuk Mubarak Dohak as signing and reading the 2026 declaration: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/04/1421666/declaration-depose-negri-sembilan-ruler-not-recognised-says-mb
claims.correction: No correction needed, except to preserve spelling consistency: Muhriz/Mukhriz appears differently in older reports.

claims.claim: Mubarak was removed for 33 offences.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810
claims.correction: BERNAMA reports the MB said the dismissal was based on 33 offences related to Telaga Undang, Waris Klana Hulu, Luak Sungei Ujong customs and traditions. The offences remain alleged/disputed by Mubarak's side, so use 'based on 33 alleged offences' unless relying solely on the state government's position.

claims.claim: The constitution lets the Undangs collectively call the Ruler to withdraw.
claims.source: Laws of the Constitution of Negeri Sembilan 1959, Article X, OCR copy via Sinar Project government-docs archive: https://govdocs.sinarproject.org/documents/state-governments/negeri-sembilan/thelawsoftheconstitutionofns1959-ocr.pdf ; Malay Mail quoting former Court of Appeal judge and constitutional lawyers on Article X, 5 May 2026: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741
claims.correction: Add the key constitutional caveat: Article X requires a full and complete enquiry and a proclamation under the hands of the Undangs and the Menteri Besar.

claims.claim: Whether three alone (without the dismissed fourth) suffice is untested.
claims.source: Malay Mail, 5 May 2026, quoting Datuk Malik Imtiaz Sarwar and former judge Hishamudin Md Yunus, including that there are no court decisions on Article X interpretation: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741 ; Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026, discussing Article 29 and unresolved interpretation: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
claims.correction: Better: 'Article XXIX appears to validate acts by at least three Undangs, but its interaction with Article X and the status of a dismissed Undang has not been authoritatively ruled on by a court.'
claims.stale_check: As of 14 May 2026, The Star reported a High Court procedural step ordering affidavits in a DKU-related challenge, not a final ruling.

claims.claim: BERNAMA and Malay Mail cite Articles 10-12; The Star cites 10-13.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026, cites Articles 10 to 12: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810 ; Malay Mail, 5 May 2026, focuses mainly on Article X: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741
claims.correction: I verified BERNAMA's Articles 10-12 citation, but did not verify from primary/authoritative source that Malay Mail cites Articles 10-12 or that The Star cites 10-13. Treat this citation-comparison as insufficiently sourced.

claims.claim: All agree: rejection rests on Mubarak no longer holding office.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810 ; Malay Mail, 5 May 2026: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741
claims.correction: Over-narrow. The state government's immediate rejection did emphasise Mubarak's status, but later legal analysis also emphasised absence of a full and complete enquiry and absence of a valid proclamation signed by the Undangs and Menteri Besar.
claims.stale_check: The analysis may have been reconstructed before the 5 May 2026 Malay Mail legal explainer; by 17 May 2026 this framing is incomplete.

claims.claim: The same Undang who read the deposal on 19 April 2026 had proclaimed Tuanku Muhriz as 11th Ruler on 29 December 2008.
claims.source: The Star, 29 Dec 2008: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2008/12/29/tunku-mukhriz-proclaimed-new-ruler-of-negri-sembilan-updated ; BERNAMA/NST, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/04/1421666/declaration-depose-negri-sembilan-ruler-not-recognised-says-mb
claims.correction: No material correction.

claims.claim: In 1967, the Undang bypassed Muhriz for the throne at 19, electing his uncle Tuanku Ja'afar.
claims.source: Tourism Malaysia official corporate site, 2009 installation material: https://www.tourism.gov.my/media/view/the-installation-ceremony-of-the-11th-negeri-sembilan-ruler ; The Straits Times, 2026, summarising the 1967 succession: https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/se-asia/the-malay-ruler-of-negeri-sembilan-can-be-ousted-his-chieftains-are-now-trying-to-do-just-that
claims.correction: Use 'was passed over' rather than 'bypassed' if avoiding loaded wording. Primary government tourism material verifies his senior title and later election; the exact age/1967 pass-over is better sourced in authoritative news and historical summaries than in a directly primary document found here.

claims.claim: Tunku Nadzaruddin (Ja'afar's son) is now the proposed successor.
claims.source: MKN government site search result, 20 Apr 2026, naming Tunku Datuk Seri Nadzaruddin ibni Almarhum Tuanku Jaafar as the newly announced ruler: https://www.mkn.gov.my/web/ms/2026/04/20/undang-undang-tubuh-negeri-sembilan-tuanku-atau-undang-yang-empat-lebih-berkuasa/ ; Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
claims.correction: Use 'named by the Undangs as successor' or 'purported/proposed successor' rather than 'is successor'.

claims.claim: Mubarak was dismissed 13 May 2025 for 33 offences against Telaga Undang custom.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810
claims.correction: State this as the MB/state government's account: dismissal was effective 13 May 2025 and was said to be based on 33 offences related to Telaga Undang, Waris Klana Hulu, Luak Sungei Ujong customs and traditions. The wording 'for 33 offences' overstates finality because the opposing side disputes the dismissal.

claims.claim: The DKU ratified the dismissal two days before he signed the declaration.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810 ; The Star, 21 Apr 2026, reporting the four Undangs disputed that the DKU collectively agreed: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/04/21/four-undang-dispute-mbs-claim-over-mubaraks-removal
claims.correction: BERNAMA says the DKU special sitting on 17 April 2026 agreed to advise and accept the Telaga Undang consensus dismissing Mubarak. 'Ratified' is a stronger legal characterisation and should be attributed; the other Undangs disputed that a collective DKU decision was reached.
claims.stale_check: Later court action on 5 May 2026 sought the DKU minutes, so the evidentiary record was still contested as of mid-May 2026.

claims.claim: The DKU sat 17 April 2026, presided by Tuanku Muhriz.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810
claims.correction: No material correction.

claims.claim: Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu declined to give Mubarak a mandate.
claims.source: NST quoting Telaga Undang Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/04/1421713/declaration-remove-negri-sembilan-ruler-invalid-says-telaga-undang
claims.correction: Attribute this to Telaga Undang Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri: she said Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu never gave Mubarak a mandate.

claims.claim: Undangs cited alleged royal misconduct from a 5 March inquiry.
claims.source: Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064 ; Malaysiakini, 21 Apr 2026: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773216
claims.correction: Use 'alleged breach in the discharge of royal duties' rather than the broader phrase 'royal misconduct' unless quoting a source that uses that wording. The allegation was reportedly not detailed publicly.

claims.claim: Institutions called the act menderhaka; JAIS and police were notified.
claims.source: NST quoting Telaga Undang Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/04/1421713/declaration-remove-negri-sembilan-ruler-invalid-says-telaga-undang ; Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026, says reports were made to police and Majlis Agama Islam Negeri Sembilan: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
claims.correction: The verified wording is that Telaga Undang Sarifah Norzaidah called Mubarak's action treasonous/menderhaka. I found no primary support for plural 'institutions'. Also, the Negeri Sembilan religious body reported was Majlis Agama Islam Negeri Sembilan, not JAIS. JAIS usually refers to a different state's Islamic department.

claims.claim: No state, customary, or judicial body has publicly addressed the misconduct claim itself.
claims.source: Malay Mail, 5 May 2026, quoting Hishamudin that there had been no news reports on the allegations or enquiry: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741 ; Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026, says details were not disclosed: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
claims.correction: This is an absence claim and should be softened: 'I found no public report detailing the misconduct allegation or a public adjudication of it.'
claims.stale_check: No stale issue found through 17 May 2026 searches, but absence claims remain inherently uncertain.

claims.claim: Daulat was defended before the underlying allegation was examined.
claims.source: Malay Mail, 5 May 2026, legal experts say a full and complete enquiry/right to be heard was required and no public reports showed allegations or enquiry: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741
claims.correction: This is interpretive rather than a directly verifiable factual claim. Recast as: 'Public institutional responses focused on the validity of the declaration and Mubarak's standing; I found no public body adjudicating the underlying allegation.'

claims.claim: The real question is not whether one dismissed Undang can act. It is whether three Undangs alone can invoke a provision designed for four.
claims.source: Malay Mail, 5 May 2026: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741 ; Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
claims.correction: This framing omits another central legal question: whether Article X requirements for a full and complete enquiry and a proclamation under the hands of the Undangs and Menteri Besar were met. It also omits the disputed status of Mubarak.
claims.stale_check: The framing may have preceded the 5 May 2026 expert explainer; by 17 May 2026 it is materially incomplete.

claims.claim: A constitution built on the collective authority of four Undangs is being tested by three.
claims.source: Laws of the Constitution of Negeri Sembilan 1959, Article X and Article XXIX, OCR copy via Sinar Project: https://govdocs.sinarproject.org/documents/state-governments/negeri-sembilan/thelawsoftheconstitutionofns1959-ocr.pdf ; Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
claims.correction: The general idea is plausible but too broad. Article XXIX reportedly allows powers to be exercised by at least three Undangs, while Article X imposes process requirements. The issue is not simply 'four versus three'.

claims.claim: No court has ruled.
claims.source: Malay Mail, 5 May 2026, quoting Malik Imtiaz that there are no court decisions on Article X interpretation: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741 ; The Star, 14 May 2026, reporting affidavits ordered in DKU challenge rather than a final ruling: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/14/judge-orders-affidavits-in-dku-challenge
claims.correction: More precise: 'No court has ruled on Article X's application to the 19 April 2026 declaration or on the full merits of the DKU dispute as of 17 May 2026.'
claims.stale_check: Checked for later developments through 17 May 2026; no final ruling found.

claims.claim: The institutions have defended the throne first.
claims.source: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810 ; NST/Telaga Undang, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/04/1421713/declaration-remove-negri-sembilan-ruler-invalid-says-telaga-undang
claims.correction: This is a broad interpretive claim. Some institutions/state actors rejected the declaration and defended Tuanku Muhriz's continuing position, but 'institutions' is vague and should be specified.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Four Undangs declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the 12th Ruler.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The declaration's legal effect is disputed and rejected by the state government; experts say the attempt was invalid. The wording should be 'purportedly deposed' or 'announced a declaration'.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Three hold office; one was dismissed 11 months earlier.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The state government says Mubarak was dismissed effective 13 May 2025, but the four Undangs disputed the MB's claim and later court proceedings sought DKU minutes. The statement should be attributed.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: BERNAMA and Malay Mail cite Articles 10-12; The Star cites 10-13.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: BERNAMA's Articles 10-12 citation was verified, but I did not verify the exact Malay Mail and The Star citation comparison from primary or direct article text.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: All agree: rejection rests on Mubarak no longer holding office.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Later legal analysis identifies additional central grounds: no full and complete enquiry and no proclamation signed by the Menteri Besar. 'All agree' is too sweeping.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The DKU ratified the dismissal two days before he signed the declaration.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: BERNAMA says the DKU agreed to advise/accept the Telaga Undang consensus; the opposing Undangs dispute that any collective decision was reached. 'Ratified' overstates a contested legal position.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Institutions called the act menderhaka; JAIS and police were notified.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: I verified only Telaga Undang Sarifah Norzaidah calling Mubarak's action treasonous. The religious body reported was Majlis Agama Islam Negeri Sembilan, not JAIS. Plural 'institutions' is not supported by the sources found.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The real question is not whether one dismissed Undang can act. It is whether three Undangs alone can invoke a provision designed for four.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: This narrows the legal issue too much. Article X procedure, full and complete enquiry, MB-signed proclamation, and Mubarak's contested status are all material.

omitted_facts: Article X reportedly requires a 'full and complete enquiry' before the Undangs can call on the Yang di-Pertuan Besar to withdraw or abdicate, and experts say no public evidence shows such an enquiry occurred. Source: Malay Mail, 5 May 2026, https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741

Article X also requires a proclamation 'under the hands of the Undangs and the Menteri Besar'; legal experts say absence of the MB's signature/proclamation is a fundamental defect. Source: Malay Mail, 5 May 2026, https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/05/simplified-why-tuanku-muhriz-is-legally-still-the-negeri-sembilan-ruler-and-why-undangs-attempt-to-sack-him-is-invalid-experts-say/218741

The four Undangs disputed the Menteri Besar's statement about Mubarak's removal and said DKU members did not make a collective unanimous decision at the 17 April 2026 special sitting. Source: The Star, 21 Apr 2026, https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/04/21/four-undang-dispute-mbs-claim-over-mubaraks-removal

Mubarak and three other Undangs filed an originating summons on 5 May 2026 seeking DKU minutes, and the High Court later ordered affidavits in the DKU challenge, indicating the matter remained procedurally alive. Sources: The Star, 5 May 2026, https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/05/negri-royal-spat-mubarak-and-fellow-undang-go-to-court ; The Star, 14 May 2026, https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/14/judge-orders-affidavits-in-dku-challenge

The alleged misconduct against Tuanku Muhriz was not publicly detailed in the reports found; this matters because Article X grounds are specific and process-dependent. Source: Malaysiakini, 20 Apr 2026, https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/773064
source_assessment: The original analysis cites only two previous T4A issues, which is circular and not an adequate source base. It does not cite the Negeri Sembilan Constitution text, BERNAMA/state statements, the Undangs' rebuttal, court filings, legal experts, or community/customary actors directly. The reconstructed cards reflect several facts that are broadly supported by BERNAMA, Malaysiakini, The Star, NST, Malay Mail, and government-hosted constitutional material, but the original source diversity is extremely weak and politically/legal institution perspectives are unevenly represented.
overall_assessment: The analysis is directionally grounded in real events but too compressed for a legally sensitive issue. The main chronology is mostly verifiable, but several statements overstate contested matters, omit key Article X procedural requirements, and use an incorrect or unsupported religious-institution reference. The strongest correction is to attribute Mubarak's dismissal to the state government's position, state the Undangs' dispute, and foreground the full-enquiry and MB-signed-proclamation requirements alongside the three-versus-four question.
